front: What does CAP stand for in the CAP Theorem?
back: CAP stands for Consistency, Availability, and Partition Tolerance. It was proposed by Eric Brewer in 2000 and proven by Seth Gilbert and Nancy Lynch in 2002.

front: What is the main statement of the CAP Theorem?
back: A distributed database system can simultaneously provide only two out of three guarantees: Consistency, Availability, and Partition Tolerance. During a network partition, a system must choose between consistency and availability.

front: What is Consistency in CAP Theorem?
back: All nodes see the same data at the same time. Every read receives the most recent write or an error. Also known as strong consistency or linearizability.

front: What is Availability in CAP Theorem?
back: Every request receives a non-error response, without guarantee that it contains the most recent write. The system remains operational 100% of the time.

front: What is Partition Tolerance in CAP Theorem?
back: The system continues to operate despite arbitrary message loss or failure of part of the system. Network partitions are inevitable in distributed systems.

front: What type of system is a CP database? Give examples.
back: CP databases sacrifice Availability during partitions to maintain Consistency. Examples: MongoDB (with write concerns), HBase, ZooKeeper, Redis, etc.

front: What type of system is an AP database? Give examples.
back: AP databases sacrifice Consistency during partitions to maintain Availability. Examples: Cassandra, DynamoDB, CouchDB, Amazon S3, etc.

front: Why can't a distributed system be both CA (Consistent and Available) in practice?
back: In presence of network partition (which is inevitable), a CA system would need to stop responding to requests to maintain consistency, making it effectively unavailable. CA is only possible in non-distributed systems.

PACELC extends CAP and states: If there is a Partition (P), the system must choose between Availability (A) and Consistency (C); Else (E), when there is no partition, the system must choose between Latency (L) and Consistency (C).
A consistency model where all replicas will eventually become consistent given no further writes. It provides high availability and partition tolerance but with a window of inconsistency.
A consistency model where all reads see the most recent write. All nodes must agree on the same value before returning it. Examples: ACID transactions, Paxos, Raft.

front: Which databases follow CAP properties? Cassandra (AP), MongoDB (CP), MySQL (CA).
back: Cassandra: AP (Eventual consistency, always available). MongoDB: CP (Consistency prioritized, may be unavailable during partitions). MySQL/PostgreSQL: CA in normal operation but become CP during partitions.

front: What is a Network Partition in distributed systems?
back: A network partition occurs when communication between nodes in a distributed system fails or is delayed. Messages may be lost or nodes may become unreachable.

front: Who proposed the CAP Theorem and when was it proven?
back: Eric Brewer proposed the CAP Theorem in 2000 at a PODC (Principles of Distributed Computing) conference. Seth Gilbert and Nancy Lynch mathematically proved it in 2002.

front: What is the trade-off developers must make when choosing CAP properties?
back: Developers must choose between: 1) Consistent but may be unavailable during partitions, 2) Available but may return stale data during partitions. The choice depends on application requirements.
